Transcript
In the job description of robotic software developer and researcher, you will find Ross to NVIDIA, Isaac SIM and other simulators. The main reason is to see the robot before putting it in a physical real world to save costs and plan properly. Now you don't have a job, let's say, and you want to get into these frameworks to build a robotic scarier and start completing the requirements of the job. The place where people get stuck is what to start. What to do with this framework? Because roster is just for communication. So in this video we are going to explore the possibility of learning Ross two in a practical way with an actual robot. So things can make sense. If roster is just communication, what are the things that are robot should be processing through Ross to and what things should it not be doing it? So let's explore in detail.

text:
Aditya Raj
2mo
“Something interesting is happening in industrial automation right now"
In just a few days, the Robotics and Automation Exhibition 2026 will bring together 350+ automation and robotics companies in Birmingham, United Kingdom to showcase the next wave of industrial technology.
But what really caught my attention isn’t just the number of exhibitors.
We’re no longer just talking about robots on factory floors.
We’re talking about:
 • Cobots working side by side with humans
 • Machine vision making real time decisions
 • Autonomous systems optimizing production
 • Smart factories learning and adapting
Companies like Bosch Rexroth, AutoStore™, Dematic Mobile Automation, Kardex, ELITE ROBOTS, and ek robotics are already pushing the boundaries of what automation can do across manufacturing, logistics, and smart factories.
The interesting question now is no longer “Will automation grow?”
It’s how fast companies are willing to adopt it.
Personally, I’m always curious to see:
 • Which innovations attract the most attention
 • Which companies are pushing the boundaries
 • And which technologies quietly become the next industry standard
#IndustrialAutomation #Robotics #SmartManufacturing
